prompt: Write a warm greeting to a new colleague in five different languages (your choice). After each, add a one-line note about what's culturally implied by the register you chose.
response:
Spanish: Buenos días, compañera. Es un placer conocerte. (A warm, friendly greeting implying collegiality and enthusiasm.)

French: Bonjour, ma chère collègue. Je suis ravi de vous accueillir parmi nous. (A slightly more formal and effusive welcome, showing respect and delight.)

German: Hello, lieber Kollege. Willkommen in unserem Team. (A semi-formal, concise welcome with the use of "lieber" indicating some personal warmth.)

Italian: Ciao, caro collega. Siamo fortunati ad averti con noi. (A warm, friendly "ciao", followed by an informal "caro" and a positive sentiment, creating an inviting tone.)

Japanese: はじめまして、お来てくださって光栄です。(Hajimemashite, okite kudasatte kōei desu.) (A reserved yet respectful and grateful greeting, typical in professional settings, welcoming the colleague's presence.)
